subject: Science
question_text: Organisms which prepare food for themselves using simple naturally available raw materials are referred to as
answer_key: autotrophs
answer_explanation: Auto means self and trophs means nourishment. Organisms that make their own food are autotrophs.

subject: Science
question_text: In the absence of which of the following will photosynthesis not occur in leaves?
answer_key: Chlorophyll
answer_explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in leaves that captures light energy required for photosynthesis.
provenance_excerpt: In the absence of which of the following will photosynthesis not occur in leaves?

subject: Science
question_text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?
(i) All green plants can prepare their own food.
(ii) Most animals are autotrophs.
(iii) Carbon dioxide is not required for photosynthesis.
(iv) Oxygen is liberated during photosynthesis.
answer_key: (i) and (iv)
answer_explanation: Animals are heterotrophs, and carbon dioxide IS required for photosynthesis. Statements (i) and (iv) are correct.

subject: Science
question_text: The term that is used for the mode of nutrition in yeast, mushroom and bread-mould is
answer_key: saprophytic
answer_explanation: Fungi like yeast, mushroom, and bread-mould take in nutrients from dead and decaying matter, called saprotrophic nutrition.

subject: Science
question_text: When we observe the lower surface of a leaf through a magnifying lens we see numerous small openings. Which of the following is the term given to such openings?
answer_key: Stomata
answer_explanation: Stomata are tiny pores present on the surface of leaves, generally surrounded by guard cells.

subject: Science
question_text: Two organisms are good friends and live together. One provides shelter, water, and nutrients while the other prepares and provides food. Such an association of organisms is termed as
answer_key: symbiosis
answer_explanation: This symbiotic relationship occurs in organisms like lichens, where algae provide food and fungi provide shelter.

subject: Science
question_text: Which of the following raw material is available in the air for photosynthesis?
answer_key: Carbon dioxide
answer_explanation: Plants take carbon dioxide from the air through stomata for photosynthesis.
provenance_excerpt: Which of the following raw material is available in the air for photosynthesis?

subject: Science
question_text: Potato and ginger are both underground parts that store food. Where is the food prepared in these plants?
answer_key: In potato and ginger, food is prepared by the leaves (shoot system) above the ground.
answer_explanation: Leaves perform photosynthesis and the prepared food is transported and stored in the underground stems.
provenance_excerpt: Potato and ginger are both underground parts that store food. Where is the food prepared in these plants?

subject: Science
question_text: A goat eats away all the leaves of a small plant (balsam). However, in a few days, new leaves could be seen sprouting in the plant again. How did the plant survive without leaves?
answer_key: The plant survived due to the food stored in the stem and root system.
answer_explanation: Plants store reserve food materials which can provide energy for sprouting new leaves when parts of the plant are eaten.

subject: Science
question_text: Nitrogen is an essential nutrient for plant growth. But farmers who cultivate pulse crops like green gram, bengal gram, black gram, etc. do not apply nitrogenous fertilizers during cultivation. Why?
answer_key: Leguminous plants have symbiotic rhizobium bacteria in their root nodules that fix atmospheric nitrogen.
answer_explanation: These bacteria convert nitrogen from the air into a soluble form that the plant can absorb.

subject: Science
question_text: Wheat dough if left in the open, after a few days, starts to emit a foul smell and becomes unfit for use. Give reason.
answer_key: Fungal spores or bacteria present in the air settle on the dough and start growing, decomposing it and producing the foul smell.
answer_explanation: Microorganisms multiply quickly by using the moisture and nutrients in the dough.
provenance_excerpt: Wheat dough if left in the open, after a few days, starts to emit a foul smell and becomes unfit for use. Give reason.

question_text:

(a) Available in the plant : _______________
(b) Available in the soil : _______________, _______________
(c) Available in the air : _______________
(d) Available during day : _______________
answer_key:
(a) Chlorophyll
(b) Water, Minerals
(c) Carbon dioxide
(d) Sunlight

subject: Science
question_text: Wild animals like tiger, wolf, lion and leopard do not eat plants. Does this mean that they can survive without plants? Can you provide a suitable explanation?
answer_key: No, they cannot survive without plants. These carnivores depend on herbivores for food, and herbivores depend entirely on plants.
answer_explanation: Plants are the primary producers in the food chain. Without plants, herbivores would not survive, ultimately leading to the demise of carnivores.
provenance_excerpt: Wild animals like tiger, wolf, lion and leopard do not eat plants. Does this mean that they can survive without plants?

subject: Science
question_text:
Can you give me a name?
Solve each of the following riddles by writing the name of the organism and its mode of nutrition.
(a) I am tall but I cannot move. I am green and can prepare my own food.
(b) I live in water; people keep me in an aquarium and feed me.
(c) I am small and I can fly. I disturb your sleep, bite you and suck your blood which is my food.
(d) I am white and soft. I grow well in the rainy season. Children pluck me from the ground and admire me. I absorb nutrients from decomposed dead parts of plants and animals in the soil.
answer_key:
(a) Tree, Autotroph
(b) Fish, Heterotroph (Omnivore/Carnivore/Herbivore)
(c) Mosquito, Parasite
(d) Mushroom, Saprophyte
answer_explanation: Identifies organisms and nutritional modes based on riddles.
